title: monoon biglandulosum
intro: Monoon biglandulosum, also known as the Borneo crabapple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Monoon biglandulosum include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating various skin conditions, such as acne and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and seeds are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
